name: PurpleLlama
tagline: Meta's open trust-and-safety tools and benchmarks for evaluating and guarding LLMs
metaDescription: PurpleLlama is Meta's umbrella project of trust-and-safety tools: Llama Guard, Prompt Guard, Code Shield, and CyberSecEval benchmarks for LLMs.
overview: PurpleLlama is an umbrella project from Meta that gathers tools and evaluations for building responsibly with open generative AI models. The name comes from the cybersecurity idea of "purple teaming" — combining red-team (attack) and blue-team (defense) work — applied to LLM safety. Its first releases focus on cybersecurity benchmarks and input/output safeguards, with more planned over time.

It is aimed at developers and ML teams who run LLMs in production and need to filter inputs and outputs, score a model's security risk, or catch insecure generated code. The project bundles several components: Llama Guard for content moderation, Prompt Guard for detecting prompt injection and jailbreaks, Code Shield for filtering insecure code, and CyberSecEval as a cybersecurity benchmark suite.

As an eval-and-guardrail framework, PurpleLlama sits between your application and the model. The safeguard models classify or block risky content at inference time, while the benchmarks let you measure how a given model behaves before you ship it.
features: Llama Guard: input and output moderation models, fine-tuned from Llama 3.1/3.2, that detect content matching the MLCommons hazards taxonomy
Prompt Guard: a model that flags prompt injection and jailbreak attempts before they reach your LLM
Code Shield: inference-time filtering of insecure code generated by LLMs, with block/warn treatment recommendations
CyberSecEval: cybersecurity benchmarks for LLMs based on industry standards like CWE and MITRE ATT&CK
Permissive licensing: evals and benchmarks under MIT; safeguard models under the corresponding Llama Community License
Covers both attack and defense ("purple teaming") so you can evaluate risk and add guardrails from one project

gettingStarted.intro: The components ship separately. Code Shield is available as a pip package; the Llama Guard and Prompt Guard models are downloaded from Hugging Face. Here is a minimal Code Shield example.

steps.heading: Install Code Shield
steps.body: Install the codeshield package from PyPI.

steps.heading: Scan LLM-generated code
steps.body: Call CodeShield.scan_code on model output and act on the result's is_insecure flag and recommended_treatment.

steps.heading: Add the safeguard models
steps.body: For moderation and prompt-attack detection, download the Llama Guard and Prompt Guard models from Hugging Face and run them around your LLM calls. See the project's Hugging Face page and the CodeShield usage notebook for full examples.

useCases: Moderate user inputs and model outputs against a hazards taxonomy with Llama Guard before content reaches end users
Detect prompt injection and jailbreak attempts with Prompt Guard in an LLM-powered application
Filter insecure code suggestions from a coding assistant or code interpreter using Code Shield
Benchmark an LLM's cybersecurity risk with CyberSecEval before deploying it
